sourceShortSummaries: Omens worsen around the Great Chasm, the first investigators vanish, and the party is swept through a white storm while clutching what gear they can keep.
The party awakens on a floating ice platform, meets one another, and begins questioning the terrified woman before Eolo triggers the guardians.
Eolo wakes the frozen wolf guardians, and the party scrambles into its first desperate exchange on the slick platform.
The battle widens as wind-shades shriek across the platform, Kalima collapses, and the first chasm wisps burst from the shattered wolves.
In darkness and drifting ice, the party revives Kalima and cleans up the last wolf, shades, and wisps on the platform.
With the fight over, Kalima names Zeyfa's Labyrinth, describes her people's old flight into it, and warns that only the beast offers any hope of escape.
The party trades introductions, compares what they know about the chasm and the missing expedition, and learns that Kalima's people send sacrifices into the labyrinth at sunset.
The group takes a short rest, tests the platform's exits, scouts the bridge ahead, and commits to entering the tunnels with Kalima.
sourceLongSummaries: The session opens with a prologue about the Great Chasm's escalating signs and the disappearance of the Vejo Vaikai investigation party sent with Kemeko warriors. The beat then shifts into the fall itself, as the adventurers are torn through a blinding white storm and forced to decide what belongings they can hold onto before landing somewhere unknown.

The adventurers come to on an ice platform suspended in blackness beneath a howling white storm, with two frozen wolf figures guarding narrow bridges. They introduce themselves, get their first look at the terrified Zimkovan woman, and realize the place feels wrong even to Ekko, who knows the chasm well; the beat ends when Eolo approaches one of the wolves and sets the fight in motion.

Combat begins when Eolo approaches the nearest wolf and both frozen guardians come to life. The first phase centers on the wolves' violent opening rush, Eolo being knocked down and mauled, and the party finding its footing as Ekko takes bear form and the others begin to coordinate under pressure.

The fight escalates when multiple wind-shades blow in from off the platform, battering the party with shrieking gusts while one of the remaining wolves keeps pressure on the front line. Kalima is dropped unconscious in the chaos, darkness begins to matter more, and the first chasm wisps appear as the wolves break apart.

The final phase of the combat is a running cleanup under bad visibility, with Finnan healing Kalima back onto her feet while the others finish off the last wolf, hunt the shades, and swat down the remaining wisps. The platform finally falls quiet after the last enemies are destroyed and the immediate danger passes.

Finnan and Justas use the brief calm after the battle to draw a fuller account from the woman, who says they are back in Zeyfa's Labyrinth and that her people came here long ago to hide from a cataclysm. She insists that no one escapes, that the only way out is further in toward the beast, and that ordinary prayers do not carry here, though Justas steadies her with Romil's token and its light.

Once Kalima is a little calmer, Finnan turns to the others, introduces himself properly, and frames her story against old tales of swamps, forgotten wars, and peoples who hid from disaster. Jrain explains the missing Vejo Vaikai expedition she was tracking, Ekko connects Eidrikas to his own past, and the group compares what it knows about Kemeko, the Kestavo, fey-like interpretations of the West Wind, and whether this place is truly the chasm. Finnan then coaxes one more answer from Kalima: when the sun sets and the new ones are old enough, the West Wind demands one of her people as a sacrifice into its labyrinth.

The party settles into a short rest while Finnan checks for magic and the storm above grows more threatening. They probe both bridges and the void below, learn that the updraft makes flying down impossible, and get Kalima's warning that all paths lead to the beast and that the maze takes travelers where it wants. After Rala scouts a snow chamber with four narrow tunnels and no obvious creatures, Justas and Finnan persuade Kalima to come between them as the group prepares to jump down into the snowbank and enter the maze.

sourceEntries.title: Portents and the Fall
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Table talk and the DM's omens-heavy prologue lead into the moment the party awakens on the ice platform.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Omens worsen around the Great Chasm, the first investigators vanish, and the party is swept through a white storm while clutching what gear they can keep.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The session opens with a prologue about the Great Chasm's escalating signs and the disappearance of the Vejo Vaikai investigation party sent with Kemeko warriors. The beat then shifts into the fall itself, as the adventurers are torn through a blinding white storm and forced to decide what belongings they can hold onto before landing somewhere unknown.

locationHints.context: swept by unnatural storm into darkness below
locationHints.notes: The beat covers the omens over the chasm and the fall before the party awakens on the platform.

npcDetails.name: Zeyfa
npcDetails.context: cruel west wind of Zimkovan legend
npcDetails.notes: Many people interpret the worsening omens around the chasm as signs of Zeyfa's anger.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.context: terrified Zimkovan woman caught in the fall
npcDetails.notes: Jrain tries to hold onto the woman as the storm carries everyone away.

organizationDetails.name: Kemeko Monastery
organizationDetails.notes: Its kenku are described as seeing the chasm as refuge and sending warriors with the investigation.

organizationDetails.name: Vejo Vaikai
organizationDetails.notes: Their mystics view the chasm as a place of elemental balance and send the missing expedition.

sourceEntries.title: Rest, Recon, and Descent
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After the platform discussion and Kalima's sacrifice answer, the DM moves the group into a short rest; the party then tests the platform's exits, learns the drop is wind-locked, scouts the snow chamber ahead, persuades Kalima to come, and commits to entering the tunnels.

locationHints.context: ice platform, bridge ends, and snow-chamber entrance
locationHints.notes: The beat begins on the platform and ends with the party committing to the first chamber of the labyrinth.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: She warns that the beast does not like waiting, says all paths lead toward it, gives her name, and agrees to come only if kept between Finnan and Justas.

npcDetails.name: Rala
npcDetails.notes: She survives the fall, recovers during the short rest, and scouts the snow chamber ahead.

npcDetails.notes: She warns that the beast does not like waiting, says all paths lead toward it, gives her name, and agrees to come only if kept between Finnan and Justas.

npcDetails.name: Rala
npcDetails.notes: She survives the fall, recovers during the short rest, and scouts the snow chamber ahead.

npcs.name: Kalima
npcs.contextHint: terrified Zimkovan woman caught in the fall

locations.name: Great Chasm
locations.contextHint: swept by unnatural storm into darkness below

locations.name: unknown ice platform
locations.contextHint: swept by unnatural storm into darkness below

locations.name: Zeyfa's Labyrinth
locations.contextHint: floating ice platform between two bridges

npcs.name: Zeyfa
npcs.contextHint: cruel west wind of Zimkovan legend
